steps.title: Spot what motivated Cole and Vale
steps.content: The passage states that while Lin explored shared genetic mechanisms and Moravec explored different ones, “the relative prevalence of convergence through shared and different genetic processes is still poorly understood.” This gap motivated Cole and Vale to evaluate both types of convergence in a single study.

steps.title: Match the motivation to the correct answer
steps.content:
Choice C (‘Cole and Vale's study addresses convergent evolution more comprehensively than the studies by Lin and colleagues and Moravec and colleagues do.’) states the study addresses convergence “more comprehensively”—which was needed because the prior studies were separately focused on one type each.
  • A falsely assumes a prior belief
  • B invents unsupported data reuse
  • D wrongly says Lin also compared both types and uses awkward wording about a shared genetic mechanism


This confirms C is correct.
